Home Projects Blog Personal Professional

Engineering Education

As Lectuer at the University of Illinois, I taught classes in computational mechanics, solid mechanics, and machine design. I redesigned course curriculum using modern best practices, focusing on student engagement in large-lecture courses and improving student literacy in computational tools like Python and commercial CAD and finite element analysis software.

Paper 1

Multiscale Finite Element Modeling

I have done work exploring implementation of finite element models which use fine-scaling modeling of representative volume elements (RVEs) to homogenize material behavior and obtain cost-efficient solutions

Paper 1 Paper 2

Atomistic models of Kevlar fibers

Kevlar polymer fibers consist of aligned polymer chains and have a high degree of crystallinity. My Ph.D. dissertation work focused on characterizing the structure-property relationships in Kevlar fibers using atomstic level modeling of fiber microstructure and the material's behavior under a variety of loading conditions.

Paper 1 Paper 2

Additive manufacturing

As a research engineer, I developed a computational model for melting and solification in selective laser melting of metal powder beds. The model was used to optimize laser scanning protocols to achieve target material properties for a given part.

Paper 1
